summary: Added a new Doxygen-picked-up Markdown glossary at `docs/glossary.md` defining common shorthand and domain terms used across the Melee decompilation codebase, such as `ft`, `it`, `pos`, `vel`, `ECB`, `AABB`, and `zako`. The PR was documentation-only and established a central reference for abbreviation meanings and naming consistency.
what_changed: Created `docs/glossary.md` as a new Doxygen page using `@page glossary Glossary`.
Added Doxygen sections for common abbreviations and terms using anchors like `@section glossary_aabb AABB` and `@section glossary_vel vel`.

Documented common decomp naming abbreviations including `accel`, `amb`, `atk`, `cam`, `coll`, `cur`, `dir`, `dmg`, `dst`, `ft`, `idx`, `init`, `it`, `kb`, `mag`, `min`, `mul`, `phys`, `plat`, `ply`, `pos`, `prev`, `rot`, `scl`, `sfx`, `spd`, `src`, `sz`, `tgt`, `unk`, and `vel`.

Defined domain-specific terms including `AABB` as axis-aligned bounding box, `ECB` as environmental collision box, controller terms `cstick` and `lstick`, and `zako` as a Japanese term used for Fighting Wire Frames and possibly other enemies.
The PR body noted that Markdown files placed in `docs` are picked up by Doxygen.
smart_moves: Centralized abbreviation meanings in a searchable documentation page instead of leaving conventions implicit across source files.
Used stable Doxygen section anchors with a consistent `glossary_` prefix, making individual glossary entries linkable.
Captured ambiguous shorthand explicitly, such as `cur` meaning either current or cursor and `init` referring to initial or initialize forms.
Included game-specific and Japanese-derived vocabulary like `zako`, which helps future contributors avoid guessing at domain terminology.
decomp_lessons: For decomp projects with many abbreviated field and function names, a shared glossary reduces naming drift and reviewer overhead.
Documentation in `docs/*.md` can be integrated into generated Doxygen output, so convention references do not need to live only in source comments.
When matching original code requires short or conventional names, documenting accepted abbreviations helps preserve consistency without expanding every name.
Ambiguous terms should be documented with all accepted meanings rather than forced into a single expansion when evidence supports multiple uses.
naming_conventions: `cstick` means camera stick, the yellow analog stick on the right side of the GameCube controller.
`init` means initial or a form of initialize.
`lstick` means left stick, the gray analog stick on the GameCube controller.
`sfx` means sound effect or sound effects.
`zako` is Japanese for trash mob or literally small fish; in Melee it refers to Fighting Wire Frames and possibly other enemies.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactics were present; this was a documentation-only PR.
Indirect matching value: the glossary supports consistent short names for fields, locals, and symbols when terse naming is useful or already established.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the PR context.
The PR had one review recorded, but the slice provides no textual review feedback.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that update `docs/glossary.md` with additional abbreviation conventions.
Search for symbols or fields using ambiguous abbreviations like `cur`, `init`, `mul`, and `sfx` to ensure usage matches the documented glossary.
Look for Doxygen documentation PRs that add more Markdown pages under `docs`.
